page: Amazon Web Services m6i.32xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 64 physical cores and 128 vCPUs via hyperthreading at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. This is supported by strong extended instruction and encryption capabilities. Memory bandwidth is strong across both small and large block read/write tests, ranking in the top 25%. Single-core performance is average, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core metrics.

While the instance excels in multi-threaded workloads, it exhibits weak performance in large-scale LLM text generation, such as the 70B model. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. Given its balanced resource density and high multi-core throughput, this server is well-suited for enterprise applications, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m6i.32xlarge is a general-purpose server configured with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 512 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a balanced 4 GB per vCPU, and provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and encryption capabilities, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database operation speeds, though single-core performance remains average. This hardware profile is highly efficient for resource-dense, multi-threaded workloads such as enterprise databases, static web serving, and software compilation, though it is limited by a lack of local storage and lower performance in large language model text generation.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6i.32xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU performance for enterprise workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors
512 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 4 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and strong memory bandwidth
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Optimized for enterprise databases, web serving, and multi-threaded applications
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
